rationale: The citing paper focuses on trust governance practices for online review platforms, while the cited paper is about utility–complexity tradeoffs in mass customization design, so topical alignment appears weak from the notes alone. However, the evidence includes only a single DOI-level match (no in-text citation context), and both studies use experimental/conjoint-style consumer choice designs, so a methodological or conceptual use is still plausible and cannot be safely excluded.
